title: pleopeltis polypodioides var. michauxiana
intro: Pleopeltis polypodioides var. michauxiana, also known as the resurrection fern, is a variety of epiphytic fern native to the southeastern United States and the Caribbean. It is known for its unique ability to survive in a desiccated state, only to revive when exposed to water. The medicinal properties of the resurrection fern include its potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, and its use in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of the plant are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and it has been used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions to aid in digestion and respiratory issues.
